section_title: INTRODUCTION AND MARKET CONTEXT
content:
Welcome to the ICT Mentorship 2022. This lesson focuses on the E-mini S&P daily chart, June delivery
contract for 2022. We're reviewing previous forecasts from recent weeks.

section_title: REVIEW OF BEARISH FORECAST AND LIQUIDITY DRAW
content:
We previously outlined a bearish order block as an area to watch. Entering a seasonal tendency, we
anticipated lower prices, which materialized. The relationship with the dollar indicated risk-off sentiment,
leading to declines in equities. The expectation was a draw down into the relative equal low, which occurred.

section_title: TRADINGVIEW PLATFORM AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT
content:
For those asking about TradingView, a comment will be posted and pinned below this video with
'TradingView tips.' Viewers are encouraged to share their tips for new users, which will be well-received,
and ICT might even learn something new.

section_title: CONFIRMING THE DRAW ON LIQUIDITY AND ENTRY CHALLENGES
content:
The market did in fact trade down into our relative equal lows, which was the draw on liquidity. New
students often prioritize learning how to pick the right order block and find the entry candle or entry point.
This is, in fact, the easiest thing out of all the learning.

section_title: PRIORITIZING MARKET DIRECTION OVER ENTRY MECHANICS
content:
Once you understand what the market's likely to do, where it's going to draw to, and why it should get
there, that's the crucial part. The majority of your studying and watching should be 'reading the tape,' which
will be outlined and showcased in upcoming live streams in May.

section_title: INTRODUCTION TO LIVE STREAM SESSIONS IN MAY
content:
Live streams will only be in May; this is not intended to be an ongoing thing. The purpose is to showcase
how to train yourself to look for market behaviors. ICT will outline what he expects price to do on intraday
charts and point out certain areas to study, encouraging observation of price reactions and expected
outcomes.

section_title: LIVE STREAM DISCLAIMERS AND EDUCATIONAL APPROACH
content:
The best thing that can happen is for ICT to get it wrong sometimes, which will disarm the expectation of
perfection. Viewers are cautioned:
- Do not take ICT's commentaries or opinions as trade advice.
- Do not enter trades based on what is shown.
- The sessions are for observing and taking notes to understand what the market is doing and why it is
likely to do what is outlined.

section_title: STRICT NON-ADVISORY STANCE FOR LIVE STREAMS
content:
It is critical not to take anything said in commentaries or opinions over the live chart as trade advice. Do not
take a trade; this will be stated every single time a live stream is conducted. You don't know the full context
of what ICT is doing, viewing, or trying to teach. These sessions will be recorded and available on the
YouTube channel for those unable to be present.

section_title: LIVE STREAM SCHEDULE AND CHAT POLICY
content:
Live streams will take place during the New York session, likely starting around 8:00 AM, to allow for chart
setup and overnight review. Sessions could extend up to 10:30-11:00 AM, though not every session will be
that long. ICT will not be interacting with the chat to avoid distraction and maintain focus on the day's
agenda. Selected dates will be announced in advance on the YouTube channel's community tab.

section_title: LIVE STREAM SCHEDULE RECAP
content:
The live streams will begin around 8:00 AM, concluding whenever the need arises, but potentially
extending up to 10:30-11:00 AM. They will not be daily but on selected dates, announced via the YouTube
community tab. ICT will not be looking at the chat during these sessions to maintain focus and stay on task.

section_title: IDENTIFYING THE DRAW ON LIQUIDITY WITH TRADINGVIEW TOOLS
content:
Revisiting the E-mini S&P chart, the area of the relative equal low was outlined as the draw on liquidity. On
TradingView, this can be visualized as a 'bullseye' (where the market is going to draw to) or using a 'magnet
icon' analogy, where price is drawn down to that level. The market did tap into this liquidity today. The initial
forecast involved a bearish order block, a seasonal tendency, and an expected decline.

section_title: HOURLY CHART ANALYSIS
content:
- IDENTIFYING IMBALANCE AND GAP
Transitioning to the hourly chart, we observe a market run that kept pumping higher, just falling short of a
high, followed by a decline and a normal pause. The market then gapped down and rallied back up.
Between this candle's low and the next candle's opening, there is an imbalance coupled with an actual gap.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING PERMISSIBLE PRICE ACTION WITHIN IMBALANCES
content:
Observe how certain candles (small piece of one, then subsequent ones) go just above the imbalance
area. This is 'permissible price action,' something you have to get used to. Look at the bodies of the
candles; they stay inside that imbalance. The wicks reach for liquidity and stops, but the bulk of the volume
is being held inside this imbalance.

section_title: 15-MINUTE CHART
content:
- MARKET OPEN DYNAMICS
The market then released to the downside. Around 9:00 AM, then specifically at 9:30 AM, the equity
markets open. On the 15-minute time frame, at 9:30, the market runs a short-term high.

section_title: 15-MINUTE CHART
content:
- ENTRY SETUP AND TARGET
Following the run on the short-term high, the market breaks lower, creating an imbalance. It then trades
back up into this imbalance. In this area, you could be a seller and get in sync with the run to get positioned
(if you are a swing or short-term trader). This could be your entry, aiming for that old relative equal low,
which is the blue line from the daily chart. The market ran down into it today.

section_title: DEFINING PREMIUM AND DISCOUNT ZONES
content:
Price traded above the opening price, meaning it's in a premium. It's above an old high, which also makes it
a premium. Any time the market trades above an old high, that is a short-term premium because it's going
into liquidity. Any time the market trades below an old low, that's a discount.

section_title: THE IMPORTANCE OF NARRATIVE OVER TRADITIONAL INDICATORS
content:
Just like overbought/oversold indicators, things can be overbought and still go higher, or oversold (in a
discount) and keep going lower. This highlights that people studying price cannot assume an indicator's
state dictates an opportunity. You must understand the narrative within market structure. Market structure
helps frame an idea, but the idea must align with the 'present narrative' why the market should go where
you think it will:
- Based on the climate
- Economic calendar events
- Volatility being offered for that particular trading session or day.
These concepts are taught on this YouTube channel and will be demonstrated practically in live sessions,
with ICT sharing his internal dialogue.

section_title: POWER OF THREE AND SMART MONEY ACCUMULATION/DISTRIBUTION
content:
The 'Power of Three' involves:
- The open
- A rally up or manipulation, creating the high (Smart Money would be selling short into the buy stops here,
near the high today, above the opening)
- The movement lower, then distributing their shorts below an area where there would be stops (Smart
Money offloading their shorts to sell stops below those relative equal lows).

section_title: ALGORITHMIC CONTROL AND MARKET FORECASTING
content:
When we see relative equal lows on the daily chart, many traders view that as a breakout. Long-term trend
following systems will see it break below those areas and want to be short, flooding the market with market
sell orders. This is a perfect counterpart for Smart Money, who were short up here, because they want to
buy it at a lower price. This forecasting ability is possible if the algorithm is in control and we understand
what it's doing, which experience should indicate is the case.

section_title: BLENDING PRINCIPLES FOR DAILY RANGE ENGAGEMENT
content:
You are taking the logic of certain principles and concepts and blending them with market structure. We're
looking for lower prices, and it should reach for a specific low. But also, how does the present narrative
allow for engagement with the daily range or 'Power of Three'? Everything taught previously applies: it's the
open, a rally, Smart Money enters near the high today (above the opening), and they get out down here at
an important low, with the market closing near the low of the day.

section_title: 5-MINUTE CHART
content:
- LIQUIDITY SWEEPS AND DAILY EXECUTION
On the five-minute chart, observe two relative equal highs. Above the opening price, there is resting buy
- side liquidity (buy stops). The market punches up through and takes these out. It then breaks down, comes
one more time back above the opening price, and then aggressively runs for the sell side below here. For
some, getting short up here and buying it back below the lows would be enough, being done and walking
away.
